data.title: Bitcoin selloff deepens, wiping out early-2025 advance
data.description:
Bitcoin's (BTC-USD) selloff this week intensified as the cryptocurrency wiped out all its gains from the first week of 2025, with many other major token prices following suit on Thursday.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) dipped 2.4% to $93.1K at 10:017 a.m. ET, down from as high as $102.7K just two days earlier and about 14% below its record high of $108.3K last month.

The intraday slump went beyond bitcoin (BTC-USD), hitting numerous high-profile crypto assets, including MarketMove (MOVE-USD) -9.5%, cardano (CAD-USD) -8.7%, dogecoin (DOGE-USD) -7.7%, internet computer (ICP-USD) -7.1% and arbit coin (ARB-USD) -6.7%. Ether (ETH-USD), the world's largest altcoin by market cap, also slid 3.6% to $3.24K.

Coinciding with the two-day price weakness, macro jitters have been mounting as the surge in global bond yields reflect concerns about upside risks to inflation, and, consequently, a tightening in monetary policy by the Federal Reserve.

On Wednesday, minutes from the Fed's December policy meeting revealed that a majority of Federal Open Market Committee members now see slower progress on inflation, noting that December's rate cut was a close call.

The stock market is closed Thursday for former President Jimmy Carter's state funeral, but bond markets remain open until 2:00 PM ET.

data.title: Thyssenkrupp says several companies are interested in buying marine unit, Reuters reports
data.description:
Several companies are interested in partnering with Thyssenkrupp's TKMS marine division, Reuters reported Thursday, citing a company spokesperson. The report followed a separate newspaper story that said German engine maker Deutz wanted to take over TKMS.

Deutz was among the companies that submitted a non-binding offer for TKMS at the end of the year, Handelsblatt business daily reported, citing industry sources. The possible acquisition would help Deutz to set up a new business area.

data.title: Libero Copper & Gold to list in Germany
data.description:
• Libero Copper & Gold (OTCQB:LBCMF) has received its listing on Tradegate Exchange in Germany.

  • Press Release

data.title: Delta Air Lines Q4 earnings: Eyes on impact of holiday season
data.description:
Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) is expected to report its Q4 results on January 10, and investors will be on the lookout for any comments on the impact of the holiday season.

The consensus EPS Estimate is $1.74 (+35.9% Y/Y) and the consensus Revenue Estimate is $14.59B (+2.6% Y/Y).

The aviation sector could be facing concerns such as fuel price volatility, inflationary pressures, and limited aircraft deliveries.

"Investors should monitor the impact of the holiday season on DAL's performance and any updates on the lawsuit against CrowdStrike," SA contributor Vinay Utham said.

DAL's mixed track record in meeting revenue and EPS estimates, coupled with recent performance, suggests caution ahead of the earnings release, Utham added.

In October, Delta Air Lines (DAL) sued CrowdStrike (CRWD) in Georgia state court over an outage in July that brought down the carrier’s computer systems and forced the cancellation of some 7,000 flights.

On the guidance front, Delta has reaffirmed its 2024 guidance and said it sees 2025 for capacity growth of 3% to 4% year-over-year, revenue growth of mid-single digits and non-fuel unit cost growth of low-single digits year-over-year.

January seasonality and earnings history for DAL are unfavorable, with negative returns in most years and poor post-Q4-earnings performance, SA contributor Leon Laake said.

Another SA contributor, Motti Sapir, however, said Delta (DAL) is well positioned to mitigate certain concerns.

"Risks like rising fuel costs and geopolitical issues exist, but Delta's strategic moves and efficient operations mitigate these concerns. Positive market sentiment and Delta's focus on high-margin services and fleet upgrades signal significant growth potential, making now a great time to invest," Sapir said.

In the last quarter, the firm missed both its top and bottom line estimates.

Over the last 2 years, DAL has beaten EPS estimates 63% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 75% of the time.

Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 6 upward revisions and 7 downward. Revenue estimates have seen 1 upward revision and 5 downward.

Shares in the company were up 49.6% in 2024.

data.title: Investors' optimism dwindles as 2025 starts off rough
data.description:
Market sentiment suggests lower optimism in the coming weeks, as investors remain cautious after digesting a rough start to the new year.

The American Association of Individual Investors’ (AAII) closely watched survey on the retail traders indicated that bullish sentiment has decreased for the fifth consecutive week.

A total of 34.7% of respondents, as per the AAII Sentiment survey, showed optimism, compared to 35.4% in the last week. Optimism is below its historical average of 37.5% for the second time in five weeks, and was last lower on April 25, 2024 (32.1%).

Bearish view about the stock market outlook have also been rising since the end of December, with the figure standing at 37.4% for the week ended Jan. 8, versus 34.2% the week before.

Concerns about fewer rate cuts by the Federal Reserve, rising inflation and uncertainty about the new presidential administration are causing investors to struggle for a clear direction. U.S. stocks ended mixed on Wednesday, as investors refrained from big moves ahead of a stock market closure and the highly anticipated December nonfarm payrolls report.

A recent Seeking Alpha analysis by JR Research said that stepping into 2025, investors are expected to face more challenges than rewards, as the market’s valuation are no longer cheap. However, Evercore analysts noted that the tech sector will lead the S&P 500 to another 20% upside year in 2025.

Neutral sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will stay essentially unchanged over the next six months, stood at 28% for the week, versus 30.4% last week.

The weekly survey, which has been conducted by the AAII since 1987, asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

The S&P 500 Index (SP500) rose about 0.9% since the start of the year, while Dow Jones Industrial Average Index (DJI) gained 0.6%.

data.title: Barclays lays out 2025 thoughts on Apple, networking and other areas of tech
data.description:
Investment firm Barclays laid out its thoughts for the technology space in 2025 in a research note on Thursday, covering everything from Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) to networking to hardware and more.

On Apple, analysts at the firm believe that 2025 will start out "mixed at best" when the company reports its all-important fiscal first-quarter results on Jan. 30. After the earnings release, the analysts said they believe there will be "some" iPhone recovery, as the company's installed base gets older, but numbers will be "choppy" throughout 2025.

"We should get more clarity on the Google TAC and expect increased pressure on the App Store regulatory front and potential noise around the high exposure to China for both sales and supply chain given the changing political backdrop," the analysts added.

Networking

In the networking space, Barclays said the sector is likely to go "back to normal" after strong growth during COVID-19 and last year's subsequent drop.

The firm maintained its Overweight ratings on Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET), Ciena (CIEN) and its Equal-Weight ratings on Juniper Networks (JNPR) — which is in the process of being acquired by HP Enterprise (HPE) — and Cisco (NASDAQ:CSCO).

Tech hardware

Barclays' analysts are a tad more constructive on hardware, as there is likely to be a recovery in IT spending amid a renewed push for on-premise and private cloud, as well as artificial intelligence.

The firm kept its Equal-Weight ratings on Pure Storage (PSTG), Dell (DELL), HP (HPQ) and NetApp (NTAP) and its Overweight ratings on HP Enterprise and Nutanix (NTNX), as the firm believes the pair is "well positioned within their respective verticals."

Tech industrials

Barclays said it is positive on the tech industrial area, as the group has more "diversified business models, select resilient end markets, and high market share positions."

It likes Motorola Solutions (MSI) and Axon Enterprise (AXON) and also has an Overweight rating on Keysight Technologies (KEYS), which should see a "favorable setup" in 2025.

Barclays also kept its Equal-Weight ratings on Corning (GLW) and Zebra Technologies (ZBRA), due in part to exposure to end markets that "remain cyclically down or weak with limited visibility."

Positive on EMS

Barclays is also positive on the electronics manufacturing services space, citing improved margins, "incremental" Ai leverage, strong buybacks and end markets that appear ready for an upturn.

It kept its Overweight ratings on Celestica (NYSE:CLS), Flex (FLEX) and Jabil (JBL) and an Equal-Weight rating on Fabrinet (FN), amid the potential for it to lose market share.

Lastly, Barclays downgraded Harmonic (HLIT) to Equal-Weight from Overweight amid "the expected depressed Broadband growth this year for the business, limited visibility within the cable end market, and customer concentration concerns."

data.title: Goldman Sachs sees almost 30% correction risk in stocks
data.description:
A pickup in economic policy uncertainty is contributing to an increase to almost 30% in the risk of a drawdown in U.S. stocks, according to Goldman Sachs.

The risk is below previous peaks, but well above its unconditional level reached in September, Goldman analysts said Wednesday. They retrieved the ~30% figure from their newly developed framework to estimate drawdown risk over different horizons. The most extreme outcomes have materialized only after the probability crossed 35%.

Most of the risk pick-up since September has been driven by business cycle scores which track U.S. growth, inflation and policy, followed by continued equity valuation expansion, GS said.

“Market variables have contributed the most to the increase in inflation momentum, with both commodity prices and U.S. breakeven inflation increasing over the past few months,” Andrea Ferrario, leading a team of Goldman analysts, said.

Since 1950, a three-month max drawdown greater than 10% or a 12-month max drawdown of more than 20% has occurred about 55% of the time if the drawdown risk was higher than 35%, the firm said. The risk shifts down to about 40% of the time if the drawdown probability was between 25% and 35% compared with an unconditional frequency of 16%.

The S&P 500 (SP500) in August underwent an 8.5% maximum peak-to-trough. An eventual recovery left the benchmark up 23% in 2024. The index (SP500) has risen 0.6% since the start of 2025 trade through Wednesday.

At the “epicenter” of a surge in policy uncertainty is global trade and the risk of U.S. tariffs, Ferrario said. U.S. President-elect Donald Trump has said he’ll direct his administration coming to Washington later this month to pursue additional tariffs on Chinese goods, and tariffs on products from other countries.

U.S. regulation and fiscal policy is also stoking drawdown risk, while European policy uncertainty at “all-time highs” has already clipped European equity relative valuations, Ferrario said.

“While the predictive power of policy uncertainty for equity drawdowns has been mixed, with elevated policy uncertainty and geopolitical risks there is potential for setbacks that are very difficult to anticipate,” he said.

Investors can track the S&P 500 (SP500) through ETFs such as: (SPY), (UPRO), (VOO), (SDS) and (IVV).

data.title: Nvidia highlights gen-AI opportunities and affordable supercomputing: Oppenheimer
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) highlighted opportunities to advance generative artificial intelligence across an array of industries, including enterprise, robotics and auto, at the Consumer Electronics Show this week in Las Vegas, according to Oppenheimer.

Keeping true to its roots, the Jensen Huang-led firm also revealed a refreshed slate of gaming GPUs, including the 5090, 5080, 5071 Ti and 5070, Oppenheimer noted.

"Blackwell AI accelerators remain supply constrained," said Oppenheimer analysts, led by Rick Schafer, in a Thursday investor note. "H200 will continue to contribute meaningfully this year (30% of mix by our work). GB200 in full production since December. Liquid cooling requirements are likely a near-term hurdle to adoption of some GB200 SKUs."

Nvidia also focused on its enterprise offerings, including a trio of AI models, dubbed NIM, NeMo and Nemotron. The Nemotron models are based on Meta Platforms (META) Llama large language model.

"Robotics and automotive are growing AI market opportunities," Schafer said. "Management highlighted the need for three computing systems in robotics/auto."

These included DGX servers to train, AGX computers to run simulations and a connecting system.

"Management also announced Project DIGITS, a small form-factor AI supercomputer that functions as a personal private cloud —targeted towards data scientists, ML researchers, students, and developers," Schafer added.

James Foord, Investing Group Leader for The Pragmatic Investor, said Digits could be the most important announcement from the event for Nvidia. The affordable supercomputer, at $3,000, could help democratize AI and possibly add billions in new revenue.

"The implications here for Nvidia are huge, and I believe the Digits could take the market by storm," Foord noted. "The value of AI is only now becoming apparent to us, and it is limited to a great extent to big corporations."

data.title: J&J gets Priority Review designation for nipocalimab BLA
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) has received Priority Review designation from the FDA for the Biologics License Application for its drug nipocalimab in the treatment of antibody-positive patients with generalized myasthenia gravis.

Nipocalimab was also recently granted Breakthrough Therapy Designation by the FDA for the treatment of adults with moderate-to-severe Sjögren's disease.
